chapter_title.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ يُقْرِضُ الدَّرَاهِمَ وَ يَأْخُذُ أَجْوَدَ مِنْهَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 117 – The man borrows the Dirhams and take the good ones from it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/320)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/320)
text: إِذَا أَقْرَضْتَ الدَّرَاهِمَ ثُمَّ أَتَاكَ بِخَيْرٍ مِنْهَا فَلَا بَأْسَ إِذَا لَمْ يَكُنْ بَيْنَكُمَا شَرْطٌ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammad, from Al Halby,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘When you lend the Dirhams, then better than there are given back to you, so there is no problem when there does not happen to be a stipulation between the two of you’.
en.sarwar: 3. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hammad from al-Halabiy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If you give dirhams as a loan, then the party pays you back with better ones, it is not unlawful, if there is no stipulation between you.’”
